title: TC001-Create a new resume from the dashboard and land in the editor
description: Verifies a user can create a new resume from /dashboard and is redirected into the resume editor.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth by appending '/auth' to the current site's base URL so the login form can be accessed.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/auth")
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link on the Kinde auth page to open the login form so credentials can be entered.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/p[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type example@gmail.com into 'Username or email' (input index 845) and click 'Continue' (button index 846).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/editor' in current_url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Sign-in failed: 'No account found with this email' message displayed after entering example@gmail.com.
- Password entry and sign-in submission could not be performed because the auth flow did not present credential fields for the provided email.
- Dashboard page was not reached because authentication did not succeed.
- Resume creation could not be attempted because the user is not authenticated.

title: TC002-Dashboard lists resumes as visible cards
description: Verifies the dashboard renders a visible list of resume cards for an authenticated user.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth page to begin login flow.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/auth")
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link on the Kinde register page to load the sign-in form (click element index 729).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/p[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Attempt to open the sign-in form by clicking the 'Sign in' link again (index 729). If the sign-in form appears, proceed to fill email and password and submit.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/p[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type the test email into the username/email field (index 873) and click the Continue button (index 926) to proceed with the Kinde sign-in flow.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Create New Resume')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Create New Resume' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'resume card')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'resume card' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- 'No account found with this email' error displayed after entering the test email and clicking Continue.
- Password input did not appear after submitting the email, preventing completion of the sign-in flow.
- Dashboard page was not reached because authentication could not be completed with the available test credentials.
- Alternative auth provider buttons (Google/Apple/LinkedIn) cannot be used with the provided test credentials in this automated test.

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:3000/auth as the test step requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/auth")
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link on the registration page to open the login form (click element index 500).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/p[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign in' link (interactive element index 500) to open the login form so email and password fields become available.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/p[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type the login email into the username/email field (index 645) and click Continue (index 703) to reach the password step.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/editor' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Job description')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Job description' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Results')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Results' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC013-Access Upload page and see upload control
description: Verifies the authenticated user can open the Resume Upload page and sees the upload entry point.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /upload (http://localhost:3000/upload) to check for the Resume Upload page and verify the presence of the upload entry point texts.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/upload")
        
        # -> Navigate to the app's authentication page (/auth) to attempt login so the upload page can load, then re-check /upload for the required texts.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/auth")
        
        # -> Navigate to /upload (http://localhost:3000/upload) to check for the Resume Upload page and verify the presence of the upload entry point texts.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/upload")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert 'Upload' in await frame.evaluate("() => document.title")
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Upload')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Upload' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Drag')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Drag' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'drop')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'drop' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'PDF')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'PDF' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'DOCX')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'DOCX' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:

- Authentication page did not load: central 'Loading...' indicator present and no login inputs/buttons on /auth, preventing authentication.
- Expected upload-entry texts ('Upload', 'Drag', 'drop', 'PDF', 'DOCX') could not be verified because the upload UI was not rendered.
- No authenticated state was reached because the login form never appeared, so access to the upload feature could not be confirmed.

    finally:

title: TC016-Attempt to generate with empty context shows validation or remains disabled

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth (http://localhost:3000/auth) because the page is stuck on a loading screen and no clickable link to /auth is present. After navigation, re-check for login form inputs.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/auth")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/ai-studio' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Job Description')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Job Description' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'required')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'required' to be visible"
        assert not await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'AI results')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'AI results' to not be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC025-Save portfolio with available username, visibility ON, and updated bio/social links
description: Verifies a user can choose an available username, enable portfolio visibility, update bio and social links, and successfully save changes.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth (http://localhost:3000/auth) as the test step explicitly requires; use direct navigate since the page currently has no clickable navigation elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/auth")
        
        # -> Dismiss the splash/loading by clicking the 'Tap to skip' button so the auth/login form can render, then re-evaluate for login inputs.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Open the login form by clicking the 'Sign in' link on the Kinde registration page (click element index 553).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/p[2]/a').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type the test email into the Username or email field and click Continue to proceed to the password step.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/article/div/div/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/portfolio' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'available')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'available' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'saved')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'saved' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed - no account exists for email 'example@gmail.com' as indicated by the visible message 'No account found with this email'.
- Authentication flow cannot proceed to the password step because the account lookup blocked progression to password entry.
- Dashboard and Portfolio pages could not be reached because sign-in did not complete.
